title: Poincaré normal form
summary: Packages the family hᵣ and the identity g₊ = r⁻²(dr² + hᵣ), with the collar and boundary identifications supplied as interface data.

mathematicalContent.heading: Normal-form metric
mathematicalContent.body: A geodesic defining function splits the compactified metric into its radial part and a one-parameter family of metrics on the boundary slices. Consequently the complete metric has Poincaré normal form.

mathematicalContent.heading: Boundary value
mathematicalContent.body: The family hᵣ begins at the chosen representative h₀ of the conformal infinity.

mathematicalContent.heading: What is verified here
mathematicalContent.body: The two displayed theorems retrieve equalities stored in PoincareNormalFormData. The identification of the symbolic slice metric with the family hᵣ, and of h₀ with the conformal infinity, remain proposition-valued certificates.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages PE metric data, a geodesic defining function r, a boundary representative h₀, a family hᵣ with hᵣ at r=0 equal to h₀, formal radial and slice metric terms, two compatibility certificates, and the normal-form identity for g₊.
correspondence.leanType: structure PoincareNormalFormData (X : PESpace) where metric; r; h0; hR; hR_zero; drSquared; sliceMetric; sliceMetric_certificate; boundary_metric_certificate; gPlus_normal_form

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Returns the stored initial condition for the slice family: at the conformal boundary r=0, hᵣ is h₀.
Returns the Poincaré normal-form equation stored in NF: pointwise multiplication by (r²)⁻¹ is the formal factor r⁻², and the parenthesized sum is the radial term plus the slice metric.
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlus_eq_normalForm : NF.metric.gPlus = scaleByFunction (fun x => ((NF.r.r x)^2)^(-1)) (add NF.drSquared NF.sliceMetric)

references.note: Source for the Poincaré normal form and the boundary family hᵣ.

limitations: The normal-form identity is a field of PoincareNormalFormData and the theorems project that field; this module does not construct geodesic coordinates or derive the splitting from a concrete metric. The collar and conformal-boundary compatibilities remain abstract Prop fields.
